docs.sui.io Analytics

2026-04-14 to 2026-04-20 · Plausible + Kapa · Run 20260421T175742Z

Overview

Executive Summary

Kapa Questions

Solid weekly audience; the 14d total of 9,448 implies the preceding week had ~5,296 visitors, making this week slightly below average.

12,326
At 2.64 views per visit, users are exploring beyond the landing page, suggesting the docs structure is guiding multi-page journeys.
Bounce Rate (7d)

Slightly lower than the 30d rate of 65%, indicating this week's visitors were marginally more engaged or arrived with clearer intent.

Below the 14d average of 168s, suggesting shorter sessions this week; could reflect more experienced returning users needing quick lookups.

2.64
Consistent with 14d (2.60) and 30d (2.56) figures, showing stable multi-page browsing behavior across time windows.
Peak Day
Apr 20 — 708 visitors / 2,292 pageviews
Monday was the strongest day, typical for developer documentation where weekday traffic significantly outpaces weekends.
Weekend Trough
Apr 18–19 — ~460 avg visitors/day
Weekend traffic dropped ~35% vs weekday average, consistent with a developer/technical audience that engages primarily during work hours.
API migration from JSON-RPC to gRPC/GraphQL dominates developer questions this week, driven by the looming July 2026 deadline, while the onboarding funnel continues to generate the highest page traffic. Move language fundamentals and PTB construction remain persistent pain points where the chatbot frequently struggles to provide complete answers.
Plausible

Traffic Overview

docs.sui.io recorded 4,152 unique visitors and 12,326 pageviews in the 7-day period Apr 14–20, 2026. Engagement is moderate with a 62% bounce rate, 2.64 views per visit, and an average visit duration of 157 seconds. Traffic is dominated by direct visits and Google search, with the onboarding funnel (install, get coins, hello world) being the most-visited content cluster. The 14-day window shows 9,448 visitors/27,678 pageviews, suggesting the prior week (Apr 7–13) was slightly busier, while the 30-day total of 20,647 visitors confirms a steady baseline. Monday Apr 20 was the strongest single day (708 visitors, 2,292 pageviews), while the weekend (Apr 18–19) saw the expected dip to ~450–470 daily visitors.

Weekday vs. Weekend Traffic Split

Weekday daily visitors averaged ~640 (Mon–Fri) vs ~460 on weekends — a ~28% weekend drop confirming the audience is primarily professional developers working within business hours.

Monday Surge on Apr 20

Apr 20 was the top day with 708 visitors and 2,292 pageviews and the lowest weekly bounce rate (55%), suggesting a content release, announcement, or community event drove high-intent traffic at the week's close.

AI Chatbot Referrals Emerging

ChatGPT (25 visitors) and Claude (8 visitors) together sent 33 visitors this week. Claude users had the highest avg session duration site-wide (541s), signaling that AI-referred users arrive with complex, research-level questions.

Onboarding Funnel Dominates Content Traffic

The top 6 content pages (excluding homepage) are all part of the /getting-started/onboarding path — install, get-coins, configure-client, get-address — indicating strong top-of-funnel new developer activity.

Dual URL Paths Creating Fragmented Analytics

Legacy /guides/developer/getting-started/* URLs continue to receive significant traffic alongside new /getting-started/* paths (e.g., sui-install: 476 new vs 185 legacy pageviews), suggesting incomplete redirect implementation or persistent old external links.

DeepBook DeFi Developer Interest

The /onchain-finance/deepbookv3/deepbook page ranks 11th by pageviews with a low 20% bounce rate, and deepbook.tech drives 94 direct referrals — together signaling an active DeFi developer cohort integrating with DeepBook v3.

7-Day Traffic Below Prior-Week Pace

The 14-day total (9,448 visitors) minus this week's 7-day total (4,152) implies the prior week had ~5,296 visitors — about 27% more — suggesting this week was slightly quieter, possibly due to the weekend-heavy distribution.

Plausible

Top 20 Pages

# Page Insight
1 / Homepage led with 1,459 pageviews from 1,121 visitors and a low 23% bounce rate, indicating it effectively channels users into deeper content.
2 /getting-started/onboarding/sui-install Top content page with 476 pageviews; high demand for installation instructions signals strong new-developer acquisition.
3 /sui-api-ref 260 pageviews with 37% bounce rate; API reference draws returning developers for quick lookups, reflected in moderate scroll depth (58).
4 /guides/developer/getting-started/sui-install Legacy install path still generating 185 pageviews — legacy URL traffic suggests old external links or bookmarks still in use alongside the new /getting-started path.
5 /getting-started/onboarding/get-coins 182 pageviews with 42% bounce; users progressing through the onboarding funnel from install to acquiring test coins.
6 /getting-started/onboarding 181 pageviews, remarkably low 9% bounce rate — users entering the onboarding hub almost always continue to sub-pages.
7 /getting-started/onboarding/hello-world 172 pageviews but 68% bounce rate; many users arrive at the Hello World tutorial and leave without continuing, possibly after completing it.
8 /getting-started/tooling 167 pageviews from 100 visitors; tooling reference is a key resource for developers setting up their environment.
9 /references/fullnode-protocol 159 pageviews with a low 22% bounce rate, suggesting node operators spend quality time reading protocol reference documentation.
10 /getting-started 157 pageviews with only 10% bounce rate — the getting-started hub is highly effective at funneling visitors into specific guides.
11 /onchain-finance/deepbookv3/deepbook 156 pageviews with 20% bounce rate and high scroll depth (62); DeepBook content attracts engaged DeFi developers actively reading the docs.
12 /references/sui-api 144 pageviews; API reference pages collectively represent a major use case, confirming developers frequently consult docs for integration.
13 /develop/sui-architecture/tokenomics-overview 133 pageviews but 50% bounce rate; tokenomics content attracts mixed intent — investors/researchers alongside developers.
14 /getting-started/onboarding/configure-sui-client 119 pageviews with 19% bounce and high scroll (66); users actively work through client configuration steps without dropping off.
15 /getting-started/onboarding/ 111 pageviews — near-duplicate of /getting-started/onboarding (with trailing slash), suggesting URL normalization could consolidate analytics.
16 /guides 110 pageviews from 82 visitors with low 17% bounce; the guides hub effectively directs users to specific developer content.
17 /develop/write-move/sui-move-concepts 106 pageviews with 44% bounce; Move concepts attract both casual readers and focused developers, resulting in mixed engagement.
18 /getting-started/onboarding/get-address 99 pageviews continuing the onboarding funnel; low bounce (24%) confirms users are following the step-by-step sequence.
19 /develop/sui-architecture/ 97 pageviews with low 18% bounce; architecture overview serves as a gateway to deeper technical content.
20 /develop/accessing-data/grpc 96 pageviews but 54% bounce and highest scroll depth (73); gRPC content is niche but readers who stay are highly engaged.
Plausible

Referral Sources

Source Type Insight
Direct / None Organic 1,955 visitors via direct traffic — the largest single source, indicating a strong base of returning users who bookmark or type the URL directly.
Google Organic 1,125 visitors with 214s avg session duration — the longest of any major source, confirming search-driven users arrive with high intent and read deeply.
sui.io Organic 485 visitors from the main Sui website, reflecting effective cross-linking between the marketing site and technical documentation.
Bing Organic 208 visitors with 80% bounce rate and only 93s duration — Bing traffic is lower quality, with users less likely to find what they need.
deepbook.tech Organic 94 visitors with 46% bounce and 197s duration; DeepBook's own site is directing engaged DeFi developers to the Sui docs for integration details.
docs.sui.io Organic 78 visitors — self-referral traffic likely from internal link clicks tracked as a separate source; 362s avg duration shows deep engagement.
blog.sui.io Organic 42 visitors driven notably by the Move Registry Interoperability announcement post (30 visitors alone), showing blog-to-docs content funneling works.
GitHub Organic 32 visitors from GitHub repositories and issues, confirming developer-to-developer referral pathways from open-source Sui repos.
Baidu Organic 28 visitors with 93% bounce rate and 0s duration — likely bot or low-quality traffic from Chinese search; minimal real engagement.
chatgpt.com AI / Chatbot 25 visitors from ChatGPT — AI assistant users being directed to Sui docs for authoritative information, a growing referral channel.
DuckDuckGo Organic 20 visitors with 60% bounce and 32s duration — privacy-focused search users showing lower engagement than Google referrals.
suiplay.sui.io Organic 18 visitors from the SuiPlay platform, indicating gaming developers seeking documentation for blockchain integration.
Twitter Organic 17 visitors with 68% bounce and 158s duration; social media drives modest but reasonably engaged traffic to the docs.
Facebook Organic 16 visitors with 50% bounce and 132s duration; community-driven social traffic contributing a small but measurable share.
evefrontier.com Organic 11 visitors from the Eve Frontier game, suggesting this gaming project is actively directing its developer community to Sui docs.
luma Organic 10 visitors but extremely high 460s session duration — event attendees referred from a Luma event page are highly motivated readers.
claude.ai AI / Chatbot 8 visitors from Claude AI with 541s avg duration — the highest session duration of any source, suggesting Claude users arrive with complex questions and read extensively.
Yahoo! Organic 8 visitors with 75% bounce and near-zero duration — Yahoo search traffic is low quality and barely engaging with the content.
okg-block.sg.larksuite.com Organic 7 visitors from a Lark/Feishu workspace, indicating an Asian Web3 organization internally sharing Sui documentation links.
Yandex Organic 6 visitors from Russian search engine with 33% bounce; small but slightly more engaged than other non-Google search sources.

Cross-Signal

Page ↔ Kapa Theme Correlations

High-traffic pages mapped to the Kapa themes they correspond to.

Page Related Kapa Theme Insight
/develop/accessing-data/grpc gRPC Configuration and Usage High scroll depth (73) confirms developers read gRPC docs deeply, matching 30 bot questions on configuration.
/getting-started/onboarding/sui-install Move Language Fundamentals Top content page (476 views) aligns with high onboarding-stage Move fundamentals questions.
/references/sui-api API Migration: JSON-RPC to gRPC and GraphQL 144 API reference pageviews reflect active migration research from JSON-RPC to modern APIs.
/onchain-finance/deepbookv3/deepbook GraphQL Data Querying Low 20% bounce and high scroll (62) match DeepBook-related GraphQL query questions from engaged DeFi devs.
/references/fullnode-protocol Full Node and Indexer Operations Low 22% bounce on protocol reference correlates with operators troubleshooting indexer and gRPC node issues.
/getting-started/onboarding/configure-sui-client gRPC Configuration and Usage High scroll (66) and low bounce (19%) mirror bot questions about CLI PATH and client configuration.
/develop/write-move/sui-move-concepts Move Language Fundamentals 106 pageviews with 44% bounce reflects mixed-intent Move learners matching frequent OTW and struct questions.
/getting-started/onboarding/hello-world Move Language Fundamentals 68% bounce on Hello World suggests tutorial completion without follow-through, matching Move onboarding gaps.
Analysis

Notable Takeaways

API Migration Urgency Is the Dominant Developer Signal

98 Kapa questions on JSON-RPC to gRPC/GraphQL migration with 46 uncertain answers, plus 96 pageviews on /develop/accessing-data/grpc

Nearly half of migration questions go unanswered confidently, signaling a critical documentation gap ahead of the July 2026 deadline.

PTB Construction Has the Highest Bot Uncertainty Rate

62 PTB questions with 32 uncertain answers — a 52% uncertainty rate, the highest ratio among all themes

Developers building PTBs encounter edge cases the bot cannot resolve, pointing to missing recipe-level documentation for coin operations and cross-transaction patterns.

Claude AI Users Are the Deepest Documentation Readers

8 claude.ai referrals with 541-second average session duration vs. 214 seconds for Google and 157-second site average

AI-assisted developers arrive with pre-formed complex questions and consume documentation most thoroughly, making accuracy of deep reference content disproportionately important.

Legacy Install URL Still Drives Significant Traffic

185 pageviews on /guides/developer/getting-started/sui-install alongside 476 on the canonical /getting-started/onboarding/sui-install

28% of install page traffic still hits the legacy path, inflating bounce metrics and fragmenting analytics due to incomplete redirect coverage.

Kapa

Developer Topics

This week saw 517 total questions across five chunk analyses, with heavy focus on API migration from JSON-RPC to gRPC/GraphQL, Move language fundamentals, PTB construction, and package publishing workflows. Multilingual questions (Vietnamese, Chinese, Greek, Korean) indicate strong global developer growth on Sui. Questions grouped by docs.sui.io category, then by specific topic.

1 certain — spot check
Q: 代币转账请求付费的grpc simulateTransaction 接口报错PERMISSION_DENIED: permission denied。 但是使用官方节点https://fullnode.mainnet.sui.io:443可以正常预估出来 请问rpc节点有什么配置错误影响了？
A: ## RPC 节点配置问题分析 您的付费 RPC 节点返回 `PERMISSION_DENIED`，而官方节点正常，问题很可能出在以下 **节点配置层面**： ### 1. gRPC 索引未启用 这是最常见的原因。gRPC `simulateTransaction` 接口需要节点**专门开启 gRPC 索引**才能提供服务。如果您的 RPC 提供商没有在 `fullnode.yaml` 中启…

3 certain · 0 uncertain

Q: 并不是落后checkpoint，而是同步后和链上状态不一致，那么我觉得应该是某个接受的checkpoint数据有问题，那么怎么快速恢复呢
A: 你的判断很有道理——如果节点已经同步到最新 checkpoint，但本地状态与链上不一致，很可能是某个 checkpoint 数据在接收或执行时出现了问题，导致本地数据库处于不一致状态。 根据知识库中的信息，**最快的恢复方式仍然是从快照重建**，因为这可以绕过有问题的本地数据库状态，直接恢复到一个经过协议验证的干净状态。 ## 推荐：使用 Formal Snapshot 恢复 Formal…

Q: 所谓的Coin可以“集成到应用中”，是什么意思？
A: 这里"集成到应用中"（store in any application）的意思，是指 `Coin` 拥有 `key + store` 两种能力，因此它可以： 1. **被包装（Wrapped）进其他对象**：即作为另一个 Move 对象的字段存储，例如放入一个共享的托管合约或流动性池对象中。 2. **作为动态字段（Dynamic Field）存储**：可以将 `Coin` 附加到…

Fee Constants in MIST and Custom Coin Units

1 certain · 0 uncertain
1 certain — spot check
Q: the sui fee is dominated in mist and cbtc in base "const SUI_FEE = 2000000000; const CBTC_FEE = 10000;" correct?
A: Yes, that's correct! - **`SUI_FEE = 2_000_000_000`** — This is 2 SUI expressed in MIST (the smallest unit of SUI), since `1 SUI = 1,000,000,000 MIST`. [[Module sui::sui](https://docs.sui.io/reference…

Q: Error%20checking%20transaction%20input%20objects:%20Balance%20of%20gas%20object%204581376%20is%20lower%20than%20the%20needed%20amount:%208729100 帮翻译成string
A: 这个错误信息的中文翻译如下： **"检查交易输入对象时出错：gas 对象的余额 4,581,376 低于所需金额：8,729,100。"** 即你用于支付手续费的 gas coin 余额不足，无法完成本次交易。解决方法是充值更多 SUI 到你的钱包，或在测试网通过水龙头获取测试币。 [[Common Errors](https://docs.sui.io/guides/developer/co…

Q: HOT是什么概念？
A: # Hot Potato（热土豆）模式 在 Sui 的 Move 语言中，**Hot Potato** 是指一个**没有任何能力（abilities）的结构体**。具体来说，它没有 `copy`、`drop`、`key` 或 `store` 这四种能力中的任何一种。[[Hot Potato Pattern](https://move-book.com/programmability/hot-po…

Sprint Planning

Recommended Actions

Doc Actions
high Documentation ✓ Confirmed

Publish a Side-by-Side JSON-RPC to gRPC/GraphQL Migration Guide with TypeScript Examples

Create a single migration reference page mapping every deprecated JSON-RPC method to its gRPC or GraphQL equivalent with runnable TypeScript code snippets.

46 of 98 migration questions were answered uncertainly by the bot, and the July 2026 deadline is under 3 months away.

↑ Reduces bot uncertainty rate on migration questions and prevents developer churn as the deadline approaches.
No side-by-side JSON-RPC to gRPC/GraphQL migration guide with TypeScript examples exists; the docs have separate gRPC and GraphQL sections (https://docs.sui.io/develop/accessing-data/grpc/index.md, https://docs.sui.io/develop/accessing-data/graphql/index.md) but no unified mapping/migration reference.
high Documentation Partial

Add a PTB Recipe Guide Covering Coin Selection, splitCoins, mergeCoins, and Cross-Transaction Errors

Write a dedicated PTB cookbook with step-by-step patterns for coinWithBalance, coin merging, shared object usage, and common ArgumentWithoutValue errors.

PTBs have the highest bot uncertainty rate at 52% (32/62), directly blocking developers from completing transactions.

↑ Eliminates the most frequent category of unanswered PTB questions and reduces support escalations.
PTB building is covered at https://docs.sui.io/develop/transactions/ptbs/building-ptb.md and inputs/results at https://docs.sui.io/develop/transactions/ptbs/inputs-and-results.md, but a dedicated cookbook covering coinWithBalance, coin merging patterns, and common ArgumentWithoutValue errors is absent.
high Documentation Partial

Consolidate Package Publish and Upgrade Workflow into a Single Step-by-Step Reference

Merge publish, upgrade, UpgradeCap management, Published.toml, dep-replacements, and versioned shared object patterns into one end-to-end guide.

23 uncertain answers across 58 package questions, with recurring confusion about new vs old Move.toml formats and multisig upgrade flows.

↑ Reduces repeated bot escalations on upgrade errors and new package system migration confusion.
Publish and upgrade are covered separately at https://docs.sui.io/develop/publish-upgrade-packages/deploy.md and https://docs.sui.io/develop/publish-upgrade-packages/upgrade.md, but a single end-to-end reference consolidating UpgradeCap management, Published.toml, dep-replacements, and versioned shared object patterns does not exist.
Tooling Actions
high Tooling ✓ Confirmed

Improve sui client gRPC Error Messages to Distinguish Configuration Failures from Network Errors

Update sui-node and SuiGrpcClient to emit specific error codes when gRPC indexing is disabled, PERMISSION_DENIED on simulateTransaction, or the gRPC port is not yet available post-indexing.

Developers repeatedly cannot distinguish a misconfigured local node from a network issue, generating 13 uncertain bot answers on gRPC configuration.

↑ Operators self-diagnose gRPC enablement failures without requiring documentation lookup or support tickets.
No documentation exists describing specific gRPC error codes for configuration failures vs. network errors, disabled indexing, PERMISSION_DENIED on simulateTransaction, or port unavailability; the gRPC docs (https://docs.sui.io/develop/accessing-data/grpc/using-grpc.md) do not cover error differentiation.
medium Tooling ✓ Confirmed

Add coinWithBalance SDK Validation to Surface Invalid Withdraw Reservation Errors Earlier

Update coinWithBalance in the TypeScript SDK to validate fundsInAddressBalance availability before transaction build time and return a descriptive error instead of a runtime PTB abort.

Multiple questions show developers cannot diagnose why coinWithBalance fails when address balance is zero, producing opaque PTB errors.

↑ Developers catch coin selection misconfigurations at build time instead of during transaction execution.
No documentation or SDK guidance describes coinWithBalance pre-build validation for funds availability or descriptive early errors; the issue is not addressed in the PTB or SDK docs.
DX Actions
medium Developer Experience ✓ Confirmed

Build a gRPC Method Explorer CLI Command for Inspecting Available Node Services

Create a new sui client grpc list-methods command that queries a node endpoint and returns all available gRPC services and methods with their current availability status.

Developers repeatedly ask which gRPC functions exist and whether their node supports specific methods, with 30 questions on gRPC service discovery.

↑ Eliminates trial-and-error gRPC service discovery and accelerates local node validation during setup.
No 'sui client grpc list-methods' CLI command or equivalent gRPC method explorer is documented anywhere in the CLI references (https://docs.sui.io/references/cli/client.md) or gRPC docs.
high Developer Experience ✓ Confirmed

Create a PTB Debug CLI Command That Inspects and Annotates Failed Transaction Commands

Build a sui client ptb debug command that takes a failed transaction digest and outputs per-command state, argument values, and annotated error locations.

Errors like ArgumentWithoutValue at command 28 and MoveAbort in command 5 are opaque without per-command introspection tooling.

↑ Developers diagnose multi-command PTB failures in minutes instead of hours of trial-and-error resubmission.
No 'sui client ptb debug' command or equivalent per-command PTB failure inspection tool is documented in the CLI references (https://docs.sui.io/references/cli/ptb.md) or debugging guides.
docs.sui.io Docs Report · 2026-04-14 to 2026-04-20 · Plausible + Kapa · Run 20260421T175742Z
